reviews.author: Sagun Express Courier and Cargo Pvt. Ltd.
reviews.text: Hoa Lu Ancient Capital – A Quiet Walk Through Vietnam’s History Visiting Hoa Lu Ancient Capital felt like stepping back into the roots of Vietnam. Surrounded by dramatic limestone mountains and peaceful rice fields, this former capital of Vietnam (10th century) has a calm, spiritual atmosphere that instantly slows you down. The temples dedicated to King Dinh Tien Hoang and King Le Dai Hanh are beautifully preserved, with intricate stone carvings, wooden architecture, and a strong sense of history. The area isn’t very large, but every corner tells a story—about early Vietnamese kingdoms, wars, and resilience. What I loved most was the peaceful environment—less crowded than many tourist spots, perfect for quiet reflection, photography, and understanding Vietnamese culture beyond modern cities. A local guide really helps to appreciate the historical significance. - Gokul khadka - Nepal

reviews.author: Han Nguyen
reviews.text: Hoa Lu Ancient Capital served as Vietnam's first capital under the Dinh, Le, and Ly dynasties from 968 to 1010. This site is more than just a historical landmark, it offers a harmonious blend of ancient architecture and stunning natural landscapes. As part of a UNESCO World Heritage site, it is an absolute must-visit for anyone traveling to Ninh Binh

reviews.author: YaMateMatty
reviews.text: DO NOT RENT A BICYCLE IT IS COMPLETELY UNNECESSARY Outside of the bike scam unfortunately we were not very impressed by this ancient site. We enjoyed the surrounding limestone formations more than the site its self. vnd 20k per adult entry 10k for motorcycle parking and we got scammed 30k per bike (I haggled her down from 50k). If you've seen other pagodas in Vietnam your not missing a whole lot if you dont come here. We were expecting the ancient aspect to be the main point of coming here but we didnt experience anything ancient here.

reviews.author: Bruno
reviews.text: I went to the Hoa Lu Ancient Capital site by bike a few days back, towards the end of the celebrations for the New Year, and there were many tourists in and around the area. This however wasn't a big issue as I could buy the ticket (for 20.000 vnd) rather quickly (I probably had to wait 5 minutes) and then go in with my own bicycle. It's also possible to rent one there for 50.000 vnd. The site is nice and quite spacious. If you come from the main road, once crossed a bridge and past the ancient gate, there is a large area with a small pagoda in the middle. The site is surrounded by large, beautiful mountains, and there is a river bank to sit and relax. A snack bar is available, with beverages, ice-creams and toilets. There are also two small but well-maintained temples accessible through little stone gates. All in all I spent a couple of hours visiting the complex at a relaxed pace.

reviews.author: Utkersh Bora ubmuse
reviews.text: Visiting Hoa Lư is like walking into Vietnam’s past and realizing it has been patiently waiting for you… without any need for dramatics. No fireworks, no loud signs — just centuries of history standing there like, “Oh, you finally made it.” Once the ancient capital of Vietnam, this place doesn’t try to impress — it naturally does. Surrounded by dramatic limestone mountains, calm courtyards, and temples dedicated to emperors who clearly meant business, Hoa Lư feels powerful in the quietest way possible. You stroll through the temples, read plaques, nod thoughtfully (even if you only half remember the names), and suddenly feel much more educated than you were five minutes ago. The setting is peaceful, green, and cinematic — like history and nature shook hands and agreed to cooperate. It’s not rushed, it’s not crowded in an annoying way, and it gives you that rare travel feeling of “I’m genuinely glad I came here.” Bonus points for the scenery making even candid photos look wise and intentional.

reviews.author: Gaurav Rane
reviews.text: Hoa Lu Ancient Capital is a remarkable historical site that offers a deep glimpse into Vietnam’s early foundations. Once the capital of Vietnam in the 10th and 11th centuries, Hoa Lu served as the political and cultural center during the Dinh and Early Le dynasties. Surrounded by dramatic limestone mountains and natural fortifications, the location was strategically chosen for both defense and natural beauty. Walking through Hoa Lu feels like stepping into a living history book. The area is dotted with ancient temples, stone gates, and relics that honor important rulers such as Emperor Dinh Tien Hoang and Emperor Le Dai Hanh. The architecture, though modest, reflects traditional Vietnamese design with curved roofs, wooden pillars, and intricate carvings that have stood the test of time. What makes Hoa Lu truly special is its peaceful atmosphere. Unlike bustling modern cities, the ancient capital allows visitors to explore at a relaxed pace while appreciating the harmony between history and nature. Hoa Lu is more than a historical destination—it is a meaningful journey into Vietnam’s past, offering insight, tranquility, and a strong sense of cultural pride.

reviews.author: Zhang Qingye
reviews.text: Visited Hoa Lu Ancient Capital during my solo trip and enjoyed the peaceful atmosphere and historical vibe, but overall it felt a bit underwhelming compared to other nearby attractions. The temples are nicely kept and set among limestone scenery, and you can feel the history of Vietnam’s first capital here, which was interesting to see. However, there isn’t a huge amount to explore beyond a couple of main temples and open grounds, and some areas felt like open fields without much interpretation or signage. A few other visitors have mentioned that there could be more context or exhibits to explain the history, which would make the visit more engaging. Despite this, the site is calm and easy to walk around, and it’s free-flowing enough to take your time without feeling rushed.

reviews.author: Manasvi Balsara
reviews.text: Hoa Lu Ancient Capital is spread across a vast area, so many visitors rent bicycles or motorbikes to explore the site. We chose to take motorbikes, and a lady approached us offering a ride for 100,000 VND per person. Since we were two people, we had to take two motorbikes. Along the way, they explained stories related to the pagodas using Google Translate, which helped us understand the historical significance of the place. Hoa Lu is truly rich in history, and exploring the ancient pagodas was a meaningful experience. After the tour, the motorbike driver asked for an additional tip of 50,000 VND per person. This felt a bit pricey, especially as it’s a very touristy place where such extra charges are common. I would suggest carrying small change to avoid any awkward situations. Overall, it’s a historically important place worth visiting, but being mindful of costs will help make the experience smoother. The entry fee is less but people offering motorbike rides feels pricey.

reviews.author: Sing Wee Choo
reviews.text: An enlightening learning journey of ancient Vietnam. Much will depend on the level of commitment and language competency your local guide if you are an English speaker. We found ours via our hotel concierge. Our guide, Ryan, was engaging and humorous. A young fellow who joked with the group to keep the long bus ride light hearted but also know when to let us rest in peace. He spoke fluent English and would share interesting stories about the place. Worth say tour from Hanoi.

reviews.author: Rith Pisey
reviews.text: We found this ancient praying place on Google while exploring Ninh Binh. It looks like an old historical site used for worship, influenced by both Chinese and Vietnamese culture. The place was nice to see, though I’m not really a history or culture expert. It was quite crowded when I visited.

reviews.author: Z M
reviews.text: This was a nice quick stop with a rich history. Im sure it meas a whole lot the the local then most visitors but after traveling through Asia and seeing so many temples, its just another one to me. Of course worth the visit no matters if your in the area.

reviews.author: Nick Qi
reviews.text: Enclosed by towering limestone mountains, Hoa Lu Ancient Capital is a sight to behold. There are temples dedicated two the two dynasties set their roots here, plus some other monuments. A worthy visit IMO.

reviews.author: Agnieszka S
reviews.text: Beautiful and peaceful place full of history. I visited on a rainy day, and even then the scenery was still stunning — ancient temples surrounded by lush greenery and dramatic limestone hills. A great stop to explore Vietnam’s first capital and feel its timeless atmosphere.

reviews.author: Wyatt McCann
reviews.text: Very cool to see the old temples and architecture. The historical significance of this area is worth the visit. I'd recommend not renting a bike. Find free parking, walk through the temples, and up the 50m steps to the first emperors tomb. Great view point from the top.

reviews.author: Emilian Jurescu
reviews.text: Hoa Lư, the former imperial capital, discovered in the most beautiful way possible—during a peaceful bicycle ride. The route was calm, surrounded by lush landscapes, quiet temples, and an atmosphere that invites you to slow down. Hoa Lư doesn’t impress through bustle, but through history, simplicity, and balance. A place where the past feels present, and calm becomes part of the experience.

reviews.author: Margareta Tornblom
reviews.text: Great experience to visit this old capital of Vietnam. We came here on motorcykel with a driver. Some people cycle. The view on the way is so amazing with mountains and paddy fields.

reviews.author: Pratik Teli
reviews.text: Historic entrance to the Hoa Lư Ancient Capital. Beautiful stone architecture surrounded by mountains. A great place to experience Vietnam’s rich history

reviews.author: Adam Ding Media
reviews.text: A beautifully well preserved ancient historical site from the first Vietnam empire, dating back about 1,000 years. The area features statues and monuments that attempt to retell stories of the past and the emperors. Interestingly, the site is still used by locals as a bypass or shortcut between towns, which was quite surprising to see. Some of the writings on the monuments have been updated into modern Chinese characters, which can be translated to better understand their meanings. It is a really nice place to walk around, observe, and admire the ancient structures. You can also take your time to pray if you wish. Would recommend spending about 2 to 3 hours here. It is worth the long ride from Tam Coc town, especially since the scenery along the drive is quite nice.

reviews.author: Athina Maniadi
reviews.text: Stunning views. 500 steps to go up, but it's worth it. Crowded? Yes. But the places you will see there are totally worth the trouble.

reviews.author: G Whizz
reviews.text: Hoa Lu Ancient Capital is an atmospheric and historically important site near Hang Múa. Walking through the monumental stone gate feels like stepping back into Vietnam’s early imperial past. Surrounded by dramatic limestone mountains that once served as natural defenses, the area is calm, spacious, and rich in history. Even a short visit gives you a strong sense of place and significance. Well worth stopping by while exploring Ninh Binh.

reviews.author: madhu anand
reviews.text: A truly beautiful and peaceful ancient temple in Vietnam. The architecture reflects deep history and culture, and the surroundings are calm and serene. A perfect place to experience spiritual energy and traditional Vietnamese heritage.

reviews.author: Herman Soh
reviews.text: Hoa Lu Ancient Capital is one of the most beautiful, picturesque places in Ninh Binh, especially at night. When the lights come on, the pagodas glow with warm yellow light — it’s truly majestic. The boats gliding up and down the river give the whole place such a vibe, and the crowds of families, locals, and tourists create an incredible atmosphere. There are lots of hot pot spots and plenty to explore. I went twice during my time in Ninh Binh and definitely recommend that anyone staying in Ninh Binh or Tam Coc make time to visit Hoa Lu Ancient Capital. The energy is amazing, and you can easily spend hours there taking in the sights.

reviews.author: Nicole Yu
reviews.text: Pretty cool spot! Visited during a day trip to Ninh Binh, so my tour guide was able to explain the historical importance of the temples and their symbols. Tickets were managed by the tour guide, but I noticed that it was just a staff at the entrance, so entrance is quite simple

reviews.author: roberto munoz
reviews.text: This is a historic place in Vietnam . A 2 hours trip from his capital Hanoi . Here you can visits were their royal past was living centuries ago . They chose this place for to hide from invasor who came to conquer . Rivers and geography make this place unique !

reviews.author: Rob
reviews.text: Hoa Lu Ancient Citadel is a quiet and beautiful historic place with old temples for King Dinh and King Le. It was easy to walk around, not crowded, and surrounded by limestone mountains. A nice cultural stop when visiting Ninh Binh.

reviews.author: Amila Siriwardana
reviews.text: Great experience... Love it... Our local tour guide explained in detail history behind the ancient capital of Vietnam Ninh Binh and who rules it.

reviews.author: Jaydeep Joshi
reviews.text: I had an absolutely wonderful time exploring Hoa Lu Ancient Capital! 🤩 The historical significance and beautiful scenery made for an unforgettable visit. I highly recommend it to anyone interested in Vietnamese history and culture. ✨ Definitely a must-see! 🇻🇳

reviews.author: Shaun Clements
reviews.text: We went on a tour with a great guide, they're all great to be fair and rode bikes there. It's a great place to learn about the 2 kings and one queen

reviews.author: Johnny Tran
reviews.text: PSA: if you’re riding a motorbike, you’ll get a lot of people trying to get you to park your bike in their lot for a fee. Ignore them all and ride right into city over the bridge. Keep going and at the end turn left. There’s a place you can safely park your bike. Cool place because history is cool. Wild to think these structures are over 1000 years old! Worth a walk around for about an hour or 2 to take it all in.

reviews.author: Maximiliano Vina
reviews.text: A little bit into the old history of Vietnam. The beauty of its nature around already pays off this visit. Not a must watch since there isn't a lot of old buildings, but a nice visit overall.
